title: These Men Obsess Over Clothes Instead of Cars and Watches. Here’s What They Love to Buy.
title_cn: 这些男人痴迷于服装而非汽车和手表。以下是他们热衷购买的物品。

paragraphs.en: We know about men’s obsession with extremely specific watches, cars, golf clubs and even cuts of meat. But what about clothes? Menswear subcultures can be deep—and very narrow. “I have been a lifelong, inveterate collector,” said Laird Mackintosh. He’s the proprietor of Thoroughbred, a by-appointment boutique in New York’s Hudson Valley that sells vintage Ralph Lauren clothing, and vintage Ralph Lauren clothing only. Mackintosh’s single-minded devotion is echoed by guys who obsess over a specific style of jacket or boot. Want to join the fraternity of geekdom? Here, four of the clubbiest niches.
paragraphs.cn: 我们都知道男士们对极其特定的手表、汽车、高尔夫球杆乃至肉类切割的痴迷。但衣服呢？男装亚文化可以很深——而且非常小众。“我一生都是一个根深蒂固的收藏家，”莱尔德·麦金托什说。他是Thoroughbred的老板，这是一家位于纽约哈德逊河谷的预约制精品店，只销售复古拉尔夫·劳伦服装。麦金托什这种专一的执着，也体现在那些痴迷于特定款式夹克或靴子的男士身上。想加入这个“极客兄弟会”吗？这里有四个最受追捧的小众领域。

paragraphs.en: There are three seminal denim jacket styles to know: the Type I, Type II and Type III, all originally by Levi’s. The Type I, the standard shape for the first half of the 20th century, was designed for outdoor work. It featured a buckle on the lower back, and “a very short body with big blousy arms,” said Neal Mello, a denim expert and the founder of Mello & Sons Vintage in Pittsburgh, who wears double-denim daily.
paragraphs.cn: 有三种重要的牛仔夹克款式值得了解：Type I、Type II和Type III，它们最初都由Levi’s推出。Type I是20世纪上半叶的标准款式，专为户外工作设计。它在下背部有一个搭扣，并且“衣身很短，袖子宽松，”匹兹堡Mello & Sons Vintage的创始人兼牛仔专家尼尔·梅洛说，他每天都穿双层牛仔。

paragraphs.en: The Type II, released in the 1950s for a slightly younger, cooler crowd, lost the buckle but added two chest pockets, said Mello. The slightly longer Type III established the modern “trucker” shape we know today, and is the most popular model among Mello’s clients.
paragraphs.cn: 梅洛说，Type II于1950年代发布，面向更年轻、更酷的群体，去掉了搭扣，但增加了两个胸袋。略长的Type III确立了我们今天所知的现代“卡车司机”款式，是梅洛客户中最受欢迎的型号。

paragraphs.en: “I’ve seen the roller coaster of vintage, but right now it’s at a crazy height,” said Mello, who notes that a Type I in “not even the greatest condition” could set you back $5,000. Cheaper vintage options, he said, include Lee’s 101J and Wrangler’s Maverick, though the smart move would be to go for a new replica. “If somebody is trying to jump into denim jackets, Levi’s Vintage Clothing is a nice way to get going,” said Mello of the brand’s lesser-known subdivision that faithfully reissues historic designs. Its 1936 Type I is made in Japan from rigid selvedge denim woven on old-style looms. The roughly $400 style even has a ‘Big E’ label—just like the original, referring to the capitalized ‘E’ in Levi’s—and the buckle-back detail, something a lot of guys go “searching for” at Mello’s shop.
paragraphs.cn: “我见证了复古潮流的过山车式发展，但现在它正处于一个疯狂的高峰，”梅洛说，他指出一件“状况甚至不算最好”的Type I可能要价5000美元。他说，更便宜的复古选择包括Lee的101J和Wrangler的Maverick，不过明智之举是购买新的复刻品。“如果有人想尝试牛仔夹克，Levi’s Vintage Clothing是一个很好的起点，”梅洛谈到该品牌一个鲜为人知的子部门，它忠实地复刻历史设计。其1936年的Type I在日本制造，采用在老式织机上编织的硬挺赤耳牛仔布。这款约400美元的款式甚至带有“大E”标签——就像原版一样，指的是Levi’s中大写的“E”——以及背部搭扣细节，这是许多人在梅洛店里“寻找”的东西。

paragraphs.en: “Having got into jeans, you eventually get into [denim] jackets,” said Tom Weimer, 37, a San Diego-based graphic designer and denim obsessive. He owns six, most from Los Angeles brand Rogue Territory. He enjoys the brand’s heavy, 13.5oz Cryptic denim. The jackets are “as stiff as cardboard when you first get them,” he said.
paragraphs.cn: “一旦迷上牛仔裤，你最终也会迷上[牛仔]夹克，”37岁的汤姆·魏默说，他是一名圣地亚哥的平面设计师，也是一个牛仔痴迷者。他拥有六件，大部分来自洛杉矶品牌Rogue Territory。他喜欢该品牌厚重的13.5盎司Cryptic牛仔布。他说，这些夹克“刚买来时硬得像纸板一样”。

paragraphs.en: Jens Burmester owns around 150 ball caps, but he considers himself more of a hoarder than a collector. “I’m not willing to pay ridiculous prices, because I actually wear all of them,” said Burmester, 50, a Berlin-based DJ and brand partnerships lead at a sneaker store. His love affair began with 1990s hip-hop acts like NWA, who took baseball hats from the field to the stage.
paragraphs.cn: 延斯·伯梅斯特拥有大约150顶棒球帽，但他认为自己更像一个囤积者而非收藏家。“我不愿意支付荒谬的价格，因为我实际上会戴所有这些帽子，”50岁的伯梅斯特说，他是一名柏林的DJ，也是一家运动鞋店的品牌合作负责人。他对棒球帽的热爱始于1990年代的嘻哈艺人，如NWA，他们将棒球帽从球场带到了舞台。

paragraphs.en: Back then, two main brands made caps for sports teams: Starter and Sports Specialties. Those labels—whose designs mostly feature a snap-back closure, lower crown, flat brim and stiff front panel—prop up today’s vintage market. A ’90s L.A. Raiders cap in good condition might cost more than $400. Burmester’s preferred source for vintage finds is the German site Vintage Cap Kid.
paragraphs.cn: 当时，有两个主要品牌为运动队制作帽子：Starter和Sports Specialties。这些品牌——其设计大多采用按扣式封口、较低的帽冠、平帽檐和硬挺的前面板——支撑着今天的复古市场。一顶状况良好的90年代L.A. Raiders帽子可能要价超过400美元。伯梅斯特寻找复古帽子的首选来源是德国网站Vintage Cap Kid。

paragraphs.en: For new caps, Burmester likes Ebbets Field, which offers $60-ish woolen, made-in-America replicas of historical baseball team caps (any fans of the long-defunct Wichita Braves?). He also praises the irreverent output of small brands such as Bushwick Yacht Club and Hat Trick NYC, which fuse sports with pop culture. Think: the Yankees logo reimagined as the “Seinfeld” title card.
paragraphs.cn: 对于新帽子，伯梅斯特喜欢Ebbets Field，它提供大约60美元的羊毛制、美国制造的历史棒球队帽复刻品（有威奇托勇士队——一个早已解散的球队——的粉丝吗？）。他还赞扬了Bushwick Yacht Club和Hat Trick NYC等小品牌的“不敬”作品，这些品牌将体育与流行文化融合。想象一下：洋基队标志被重新构想成《宋飞正传》的片头。

paragraphs.en: First designed for 1930s miners, the Iron Ranger is one of Minnesota shoemaker Red Wing’s flagship products, and the definitive heritage workwear boot. The ankle-high leather lace-up boasts a distinctive top cap, and its Goodyear-welted construction allows for easy resoling.
paragraphs.cn: Iron Ranger最初是为1930年代的矿工设计的，是明尼苏达州鞋匠Red Wing的旗舰产品之一，也是最具代表性的复古工装靴。这款及踝高筒系带皮靴拥有独特的鞋头盖，其固特异沿条结构便于轻松更换鞋底。

paragraphs.en: Breaking in the boots is a rite of passage. “They can be unforgiving,” said Weimer, the graphic designer, who’s also a Red Wings fan. He owns three pairs of Red Wings, two of which are $350 Iron Rangers: one in pale oiled suede, the other in chestnut-hued harness leather, a style that Weimer calls “iconic” in the Red Wing universe. He wears them hiking, to the office, “anywhere other than a marathon.” He appreciates their scratches and scuffs, which tell a story that isn’t “reliant on logos,” and thinks “men are drawn to things that are earned, rather than styled.”
paragraphs.cn: 磨合靴子是一种成人礼。“它们可能很不舒服，”平面设计师魏默说，他也是Red Wings的粉丝。他拥有三双Red Wings，其中两双是350美元的Iron Rangers：一双是浅色油鞣麂皮，另一双是栗色马具皮革，魏默称这种款式在Red Wing的世界里是“标志性的”。他穿着它们去徒步、去办公室，“除了马拉松之外的任何地方”。他欣赏它们磨损的划痕和擦伤，这些痕迹讲述着一个“不依赖于标志”的故事，他认为“男士们更喜欢通过努力获得而非刻意造型的东西”。

paragraphs.en: Tips on breaking in new Iron Rangers can be found on the r/RedWingShoes Reddit page. Some of its 78,000 members suggest plans of attack, including wearing the boots nonstop (even in bed) for the first three days. Weimer said it’s worth the effort. Red Wings “only become yours through time and wear.”
paragraphs.cn: 关于如何磨合新Iron Rangers的技巧可以在r/RedWingShoes的Reddit页面上找到。其78,000名成员中的一些人提出了“攻击计划”，包括在前三天不间断地穿着靴子（甚至在床上）。魏默说这值得付出努力。Red Wings“只有通过时间和穿着才能真正属于你”。

paragraphs.en: “I am one of those Ralph Lauren nerds, for lack of a better word,” said Mackintosh, who fell for the brand when he worked at a Ralph Lauren store in the ’80s. He was seduced by Lauren’s various sartorial personas: the cowboy, the Wall Street banker, the English aristocrat, the Nantucket sailor. When the pandemic struck, Mackintosh, an actor by trade, started selling pieces from his own collection. That side hustle turned into Thoroughbred. His customer base is mainly men, of all ages. Some are interested in ’90s Polo Sport, others only in RRL, the label’s heritage workwear sub-brand. Mackintosh says he might get a guy looking to complete his collection of Polo Bear sweaters, a signature pullover with a stitched teddy bear motif that comes in countless colors and materials.
paragraphs.cn: “我就是那种拉尔夫·劳伦的‘书呆子’，如果找不到更好的词来形容的话，”麦金托什说，他在80年代在拉尔夫·劳伦商店工作时就爱上了这个品牌。他被劳伦的各种着装风格所吸引：牛仔、华尔街银行家、英国贵族、楠塔基特水手。当疫情来袭时，麦金托什，一个职业演员，开始出售他自己收藏的物品。这个副业变成了Thoroughbred。他的客户群主要是男性，涵盖所有年龄段。有些人对90年代的Polo Sport感兴趣，另一些人则只对RRL感兴趣，这是该品牌的复古工装子品牌。麦金托什说，他可能会遇到一个想集齐Polo Bear毛衣的顾客，这是一种带有缝制泰迪熊图案的标志性套头衫，有无数种颜色和材质。

paragraphs.en: The foremost “grail” at Mackintosh’s store? A voluminous, double breasted, camel-hair Polo overcoat. Costing around $2,000, the beige design has “become a symbol of something aspirational in the same way guys want a Rolex Submariner,” he said.
paragraphs.cn: 麦金托什店里最重要的“圣杯”是什么？一件宽松、双排扣的驼绒Polo大衣。他说，这款米色设计售价约2000美元，“已经成为一种抱负的象征，就像男士们想要一块劳力士潜航者一样。”

paragraphs.en: Appeared in the January 31, 2026, print edition as 'You Big (Menswear) Nerd!'.
paragraphs.cn: 刊登于2026年1月31日印刷版，标题为《你这个大（男装）书呆子！》。

vocabulary.meaning_en: to be preoccupied with or haunted by a particular thought, feeling, or desire.
vocabulary.example: We know about men’s obsession with extremely specific watches, cars, golf clubs and even cuts of meat.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们都知道男士们对极其特定的手表、汽车、高尔夫球杆乃至肉类切割的痴迷。

vocabulary.meaning_en: the owner of a business, or a holder of property.
vocabulary.example: He’s the proprietor of Thoroughbred, a by-appointment boutique in New York’s Hudson Valley that sells vintage Ralph Lauren clothing, and vintage Ralph Lauren clothing only.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他是Thoroughbred的老板，这是一家位于纽约哈德逊河谷的预约制精品店，只销售复古拉尔夫·劳伦服装。

vocabulary.meaning_en: a small shop selling fashionable clothes or accessories.
vocabulary.example: He’s the proprietor of Thoroughbred, a by-appointment boutique in New York’s Hudson Valley that sells vintage Ralph Lauren clothing, and vintage Ralph Lauren clothing only.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他是Thoroughbred的老板，这是一家位于纽约哈德逊河谷的预约制精品店，只销售复古拉尔夫·劳伦服装。

vocabulary.meaning_en: strongly influencing later developments; original and important.
vocabulary.meaning_cn: 具有开创性的；有深远影响的；重要的
vocabulary.example: There are three seminal denim jacket styles to know: the Type I, Type II and Type III, all originally by Levi’s.
vocabulary.example_cn: 有三种重要的牛仔夹克款式值得了解：Type I、Type II和Type III，它们最初都由Levi’s推出。

vocabulary.meaning_en: an exact copy or model of something, especially one on a smaller scale.
vocabulary.example: Cheaper vintage options, he said, include Lee’s 101J and Wrangler’s Maverick, though the smart move would be to go for a new replica.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他说，更便宜的复古选择包括Lee的101J和Wrangler的Maverick，不过明智之举是购买新的复刻品。

vocabulary.meaning_en: a part of a larger whole; a smaller division of an area or group.
vocabulary.example: “If somebody is trying to jump into denim jackets, Levi’s Vintage Clothing is a nice way to get going,” said Mello of the brand’s lesser-known subdivision that faithfully reissues historic designs.
vocabulary.example_cn: “如果有人想尝试牛仔夹克，Levi’s Vintage Clothing是一个很好的起点，”梅洛谈到该品牌一个鲜为人知的子部门，它忠实地复刻历史设计。

vocabulary.meaning_en: the best or most important thing owned or produced by a particular organization.
vocabulary.meaning_cn: 旗舰产品；最重要的产品
vocabulary.example: First designed for 1930s miners, the Iron Ranger is one of Minnesota shoemaker Red Wing’s flagship products, and the definitive heritage workwear boot.
vocabulary.example_cn: Iron Ranger最初是为1930年代的矿工设计的，是明尼苏达州鞋匠Red Wing的旗舰产品之一，也是最具代表性的复古工装靴。

vocabulary.meaning_en: characteristic of one person or thing, and so serving to distinguish it from others.
vocabulary.meaning_cn: 独特的；有特色的；与众不同的
vocabulary.example: The ankle-high leather lace-up boasts a distinctive top cap, and its Goodyear-welted construction allows for easy resoling.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这款及踝高筒系带皮靴拥有独特的鞋头盖，其固特异沿条结构便于轻松更换鞋底。

vocabulary.meaning_en: relating to or characterized by aspiration; having a strong desire to achieve something high or great.
vocabulary.meaning_cn: 抱负的；有志向的；令人向往的
vocabulary.example: Costing around $2,000, the beige design has “become a symbol of something aspirational in the same way guys want a Rolex Submariner,” he said.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他说，这款米色设计售价约2000美元，“已经成为一种抱负的象征，就像男士们想要一块劳力士潜航者一样。”

vocabulary.meaning_en: relating to tailoring, clothes, or style of dress.
vocabulary.meaning_cn: 裁缝的；服装的；衣着风格的
vocabulary.example: He was seduced by Lauren’s various sartorial personas: the cowboy, the Wall Street banker, the English aristocrat, the Nantucket sailor.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他被劳伦的各种着装风格所吸引：牛仔、华尔街银行家、英国贵族、楠塔基特水手。
